reviews.review_msg: make good connections with the TA's is all I can say cause sometimes they hint at what could be on the exams. I passed the final portion but failed the lab exam and now I have to retake it but def not taking it with her. U gotta memorize everything on the slides word for word bc the TA's look for keywords. Don't do it to urself honestly.

You MUST pass the lab exam to pass this course (if you fail lab exam you fail the course), We took the lab exam 4 or so weeks before final and if she purposefully said that she will not reveal the Lab grades until finals are out so you waste your time and you cannot withdraw from the course.

Incredibly hard marker, you have to have lecture slides memorized word for word or you'll get marks taken off. You have to work really hard to get a good mark in her class. The keywords are very, very important.

Final exam and lab finals were very difficult. Tutorials definitely are a lifesaver if you want to do well in the course. Lectures are not the most interesting but are crucial to the midterm and final exam. She is super caring but do not let that deceive you into thinking her tests are easy!
Failed both lab and lecture finals. Wouldn't recommend this prof at all. Save ur sanity.

TA's were not helpful. Marking was very brutal now I have to retake this course. Just don't take it with her tbh. TA marked so bad for the lab final. The prof just needs to get a hang of everything cause students are paying for this course.

I did the final and the lab exam recently and just got my results. I thought I had done pretty good like got at least 70% on both finds out I barely passed the lab and failed my final. I ended up with a 61% which is not good but at least I passed. She is a bad prof, insanely lecture heavy and very difficult, The Ta's are not useful.

Her new TA was not good. she did not know anything to put it in the simplest form. marking is extremely harsh. She told us that final would be majority of the stuff from after the midterm… not true. There was barely any new stuff.

The course material is presented through vague lecture slides that are difficult to follow. There is very little constructive feedback provided on major assignments, making it hard to improve. The grading expectations feel disconnected from the lecture content, and the overall support for students is minimal. She's nice ig.
She's okay. Not good but not bad either. Her tests are okay in terms of questions but you need to be very specific with your answers to even gain full marks. Very lecture heavy but she's very nice!

During midterm student asked if there would be punnet square on the test so if they should bring pencil. The prof replied "there will not be punnet squares or questions that require anything of that sort" on the test there was in fact punnet square. Mind you the student asked the question 10 minutes before midterm.

She's really easy to talk to and she provides a lot of materials. Textbook is unnecessary, her handouts are pretty informative and any personal notes as well. Be very specific in your wording on exams because she looks for keywords in order to provide a mark. The lab exam is chaotic, make sure you prepare well for it.

She was an awesome professor to have as a first year student. The exams are fair and decent if you know your material. Textbook isn't required, just make sure to pay attention in lectures and go through lecture slides in your own time. I recommend making your own notes for lectures. The lab final exam was very hard so start studying weeks prior.

Attendance to lectures is mandatory. She makes you enter a code every lecture so you can get participation marks. If you're late you lose marks. The code is like before the start of class to the time it starts. So you better hope to arrive on time.

she isn't that bad you just have to be accountable of your own learning. lecture slides aren't the best, packed with info that is unnecessary at times. midterm and exam is hella easy if you know the material. really kind and caring person though
never take this prof, the final exam was horrible and not based on anything she even taught.

This class was way too hard as the lectures slides were confusing and the labs were useless for us to get prepared for the exams. Overall, a tough grader and the tests don't make sense. I would recommend not taking her!!!!!!
talks about random stuff, not everything she talks about is on lectures, tests have nothing to do with lectures, practice midterms she provides are useless. Avoid taking her at all costs.
Final had bad mcqs (like mostly ‘how many answers are correct' type thing) and short answer questions were more like long answer questions. She's nice and objective list helped.
Shes middle of the pack in terms of professors. I got a grade worser than what I wanted, so attend office hours with her more often and get 1 on 1 talks.

Ivona is a good lecturer, but is difficult because of how she grades things. Her exams have many short answer questions that are generally somewhat open ended, but the answers she wants are far more specific than the questions ask for. This makes it kind of feel like you are being graded by invisible, unknowable criteria. Grade scaling present.
watch out, too many lecture slides and goes by content too fast.

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS. I did not listen to people when they said don't take it but I wish I listened. Her test were not related to the material and VERY DIFFICULT. Each lecture had 70+ slides. She gives test study guides but the tests are the exact opposite. Lab final was difficult as well. Try as hard as you want but that A will never be seen.

Easier than BISC 101. Office hours don't help. Spends a lot of time on useless stuff during lectures. If she emphasizes something, it doesn't mean it'll be on the tests and vice versa. Overexplains and ends up complicating the topic, eventually confusing you so it's better to ask TAs for help. Lab exam is easier than 101 as it is open book.

If she spends a lot of time on something, it doesn't mean that it is necessarily important. Correct terminology is very important for the lecture exams. Lab exam is hard but doable if you start prep on time (I didn't). No point in buying the textbook. Going to lectures doesn't help much, but she has in-class quizzes that are worth 5%.

ivona was really engaging. there were in class quizzes which made attendance mandatory. also weekly at home quizzes that ‘forced' you to review the material each week. TAs were really helpful, please talk to them if u need (ivona was also extremely approachable). labs weren't mandatory attendance but i recommend going to strength ur understanding.

Her labs were fun and her lectures were interactive which made it easier to follow along with topics. Definitely cares about het students and also got us all Tim bits one class. The midterm covers the first 1/2 of the course and the final covers the 1/2 half of the course, but the midterm was way easier than the final so don't be fooled.

The class is challenging but manageable with dedication. While the professor's teaching style may not suit everyone, utilizing resources like the textbook and Unstuckstudy can help you succeed. Attend classes and stay on top of the material to earn a good grade.

Dr. Mladenovic was one of the first profs I had at SFU, and she was honestly really great. She is extremely caring and always did her very best to engage us in the material she was teaching, even buying the entire class of 200 donuts on one occasion. She is very sweet and the lectures/exams were fair, but you definitely need to study hard.

Dr. M is very accommodating. We had a TA strike so she made the midterm open book and MC. However, she is a very tough grader and expects us to use correct and precise key words otherwise we don't get marks for the question. This makes it harder to do well on short answer questions in her exams... I believe the course was scaled though
Her class was quite boring and she had all these extra slides that were not covered in lecture, which just me the course hell. In terms of her marking it was super specific and not super fair.
For all first years I would say avoid this prof and instead of taking this class at the Surrey Campus do yourselves a favour and take it in Burnaby.

Very friendly and approachable to questions/feedback. Lectures are content heavy in the span of 50min. but if you review and catch up its not too difficult. She also has handouts that sum up her lectures, self tests after each unit for practice and the textbook for extra help. Tests are fair and the lab exam is easy if you do the work
Would not recommend, the lab exam was brutal. Boring lectures as well, slides also horrible.
Do not take an online class with her. She could not give 2 s***s about the 40 online students she had. Given audio recordings and basically sends you off to do whatever.
